verses.commentary: After this, Jesus went around in Galilee. He did not want to go about in Judea because the Jewish leaders there were looking for a way to kill Him. Strategic avoidance. Timing matters. Jesus protects His life until the appointed hour.
The Festival of Tabernacles was near. Sukkot, celebrating wilderness wandering and God's provision. Jesus will fulfill its symbolism.
Jesus' brothers said to Him, Leave Galilee and go to Judea, so that Your disciples there may see the works You do. Pressure from family. They want public validation. Fame-seeking advice.
No one who wants to become a public figure acts in secret. Since You are doing these things, show Yourself to the world. Worldly ambition. Political calculation. They think like unbelievers.
For even His own brothers did not believe in Him. Painful note. Family rejection. Closest relationships miss His identity. Familiarity breeds unbelief.
My time is not yet here; for you any time will do. Divine timetable versus human urgency. Jesus operates on the Father's schedule, not social pressure.
The world cannot hate you, but it hates Me because I testify that its works are evil. Moral confrontation creates hostility. Jesus' righteousness exposes sin. Truth provokes hatred.
I am not going up to this festival, because My time has not yet fully come. Not yet means timing, not refusal. His hour governs all decisions.
After He had said this, He stayed in Galilee. Apparent refusal. Delay is not denial.
However, after His brothers had left for the festival, He went also, not publicly, but in secret. Strategic timing. He goes on His terms, His way. Sovereignty in movement.
The Jewish leaders were watching for Jesus and asking, Where is He? Hostile surveillance. They seek to arrest, not worship.
Among the crowds there was widespread whispering about Him. Some said, He is a good man. Others replied, No, He deceives the people. Division over Jesus' identity. No neutral ground.
But no one would say anything publicly about Him for fear of the leaders. Terror silences truth. Political intimidation controls speech. Courage required to confess Christ.
Not until halfway through the festival did Jesus go up to the temple courts and begin to teach. Dramatic entrance. Midpoint arrival. Public teaching in official space.
The Jews there were amazed and asked, How did this man get such learning without having been taught? No rabbinic credentials. Unauthorized wisdom. They question His education, not His truth.
My teaching is not My own. It comes from the One who sent Me. Divine origin. Jesus does not invent but reveals. Authority derived from the Father.

Anyone who chooses to do the will of God will find out whether My teaching comes from God or whether I speak on My own. Moral test for truth. Willing obedience produces recognition. Doing precedes full knowing.

Whoever speaks on their own does so to gain personal glory, but He who seeks the glory of the One who sent Him is a man of truth. Motive determines authenticity. Self-promotion versus God-exaltation. Jesus seeks the Father's honor alone.
Has not Moses given you the law? Yet not one of you keeps the law. Why are you trying to kill Me? Their hypocrisy exposed. They claim Moses but murder the Messiah. Law-keepers are law-breakers.
You are demon-possessed. Who is trying to kill You? The crowd denies what leaders plot. Ignorance or deflection. They dismiss His claim as paranoia.
I did one miracle, and you are all amazed. Reference to John 5, healing on Sabbath. One sign still reverberates. Controversy lingers.
Yet, because Moses gave you circumcision, you circumcise a boy on the Sabbath. Rabbinic logic. Circumcision on the eighth day, even if Sabbath. Law permits Sabbath work for covenant sign.

Now if a boy can be circumcised on the Sabbath so that the law of Moses may not be broken, why are you angry with Me for healing a man's whole body on the Sabbath? Lesser to greater argument. Partial restoration permitted; why not total? Their logic is inconsistent.
Stop judging by mere appearances, but instead judge correctly. Superficial judgment versus true discernment. Look deeper. Evaluate rightly.
At that point some of the people of Jerusalem began to ask, Isn't this the man they are trying to kill? Open secret. Everyone knows the plot. Yet He teaches publicly.
Here He is, speaking publicly, and they are not saying a word to Him. Have the authorities really concluded that He is the Messiah? Confusion. Why no arrest? Does silence mean acceptance?

But we know where this man is from; when the Messiah comes, no one will know where He is from. Misguided assumption. They think Messiah's origin will be mysterious. Ironically, they don't know Jesus' true origin.

Yes, you know Me, and you know where I am from. I am not here on My own authority, but He who sent Me is true. You do not know Him. Ironic agreement. They know Nazareth, miss heaven. True origin unknown.
But I know Him because I am from Him and He sent Me. Intimate knowledge. Jesus comes from the Father's presence. Mission and origin unified.
At this they tried to seize Him, but no one laid a hand on Him, because His hour had not yet come. Divine protection. Timing trumps human intention. They cannot touch Him until appointed time.
Still, many in the crowd believed in Him. They said, When the Messiah comes, will He perform more signs than this man? Signs produce faith. Logic: greater works unlikely. Jesus must be Messiah.

The Pharisees heard the crowd whispering such things about Him. Then the chief priests and the Pharisees sent temple guards to arrest Him. Growing belief triggers opposition. Leaders act to stop the movement.
I am with you for only a short time, and then I am going to the One who sent Me. Limited opportunity. His presence is temporary. Return to the Father imminent.
You will look for Me, but you will not find Me; and where I am, you cannot come. Future regret. They will seek too late. Heaven is inaccessible to unbelievers.

The Jews said to one another, Where does this man intend to go that we cannot find Him? Will He go where our people live scattered among the Greeks? Ironic prophecy. Jesus will go to Gentiles through the church, not physically. They speak truth unknowingly.
What did He mean when He said, You will look for Me, but you will not find Me, and Where I am, you cannot come? Confusion persists. Spiritual truth sounds like riddle. Heaven beyond their grasp.

On the last and greatest day of the festival, Jesus stood and said in a loud voice, Let anyone who is thirsty come to Me and drink. Climactic moment. Water ceremony on Tabernacles. Jesus offers Himself as true water. Universal invitation.

Whoever believes in Me, as Scripture has said, rivers of living water will flow from within them. Internal spring. Not just receiving but overflowing. Believers become sources. Isaiah 58:11, Zechariah 14:8 echoed.

By this He meant the Spirit, whom those who believed in Him were later to receive. Up to that time the Spirit had not been given, since Jesus had not yet been glorified. John's explanation. The Spirit awaits glorification. Pentecost depends on resurrection and ascension.
On hearing His words, some of the people said, Surely this man is the Prophet. Deuteronomy 18:15. Prophet like Moses. Recognition dawns.
Others said, He is the Messiah. Still others asked, How can the Messiah come from Galilee? Division over geography. Davidic origin expected from Bethlehem. They don't know He was born there.

Does not Scripture say that the Messiah will come from David's descendants and from Bethlehem, the town where David lived? Micah 5:2. Correct theology, wrong conclusion. They don't investigate His actual birthplace.
Thus the people were divided because of Jesus. Inevitable split. Jesus forces decision. No neutrality possible.
Some wanted to seize Him, but no one laid a hand on Him. Divine restraint. They desire but cannot execute. God's sovereignty protects.
Finally the temple guards went back to the chief priests and the Pharisees, who asked them, Why didn't you bring Him in? Mission failed. Guards return empty-handed. Leaders demand explanation.
No one ever spoke the way this man does. Unprecedented authority. Guards overwhelmed by His words. Truth captivates even opponents.
You mean He has deceived you also? Leaders scoff. Gullibility charged. They interpret conviction as deception.
Have any of the rulers or of the Pharisees believed in Him? Appeal to authority. Elite consensus against Jesus. They assume education guarantees truth.
No! But this mob that knows nothing of the law, there is a curse on them. Contempt for common people. Spiritual elitism. They curse those whom God blesses.
Nicodemus, who had gone to Jesus earlier and who was one of their own number, asked. Nicodemus returns. Previously secret seeker now speaks. Character reference: John 3.
Does our law condemn a man without first hearing him to find out what he has been doing? Legal principle. Due process required. Fair hearing demanded. Courageous minority voice.

Are you from Galilee, too? Look into it, and you will find that a prophet does not come out of Galilee. Geographic prejudice. They mock Nicodemus. Actually, prophets did come from Galilee: Jonah, Nahum, possibly Hosea.
Then they all went home. Stalemate. Council disperses without resolution. Tension unresolved. Next confrontation looms.
